candidates.why: Most direct top-line revenue label; use as primary if populated in some years.
Fallback top-line revenue label when Revenue is missing; more appropriate than balancing or interest-related rows.
Not a revenue line; appears to be netted finance/other item, so not appropriate except as rejected candidate.
Balancing row, explicitly excluded when real revenue rows exist.

variables.notes: Using revenue labels as a priority list: prefer 'Revenue', then 'Total Revenue'.

candidates.why: Best match for direct production/service costs on the income statement.
Inventory asset, not an income statement expense; not suitable for COGS.

variables.notes: Selected the income-statement production cost line as COGS.

candidates.why: Direct SG&A component.
Direct SG&A component.
Administrative-related, but capitalization makes it less clearly period SG&A expense.
Headcount metric, not an expense row.

variables.notes: Candidate list contains valid SG&A rows but appears incomplete for overhead coverage. Per rule for mostly empty explicit SG&A rows, included 'Administrative expenses' and 'Selling Expenses' plus closest overhead buckets from the full preview: 'Other Operating Expense' and 'Personal Costs'. Manual review needed because these added rows were outside the candidate list. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Other Operating Expense, [Income Statement] Personal Costs

candidates.why: Development-related expense line; plausible R&D proxy.
Capitalized development cost is not ideal as expense, but may reflect R&D-related reporting label over time.
Most explicit dedicated R&D expense label.
Balance-sheet asset/project row, not an expense line.
Balance-sheet asset row, not an expense line.

variables.notes: Priority list uses explicit R&D expense first, then development cost line as fallback if labels change over time.

candidates.why: Could contain net interest/other finance item, but less preferred than explicit interest expense.
Explicit interest expense label; best match.

variables.notes: Using explicit interest expense first, with netted interest-related row as fallback.

candidates.why: Asset/investment row, not book equity.
Best available equity line; no separate parent-only equity line is shown.
Includes minority interest if present; less preferred for BE because it risks double counting with MIB.
Balancing row, not a substantive equity measure.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Balancing row, not equity.

variables.notes: Selected 'Total Equity' because no owner/parent-only equity line is available. To avoid double counting, MIB is left empty.

candidates.why: Aggregate equity including minority interest, not a standalone NCI line; should not be used if BE uses Total Equity.

variables.notes: No standalone minority interest / non-controlling interest row is present. Since BE uses 'Total Equity', MIB is set to empty to avoid double counting.
